Description: Technology type (SHS), installed capacity, and battery storage.
Wall, floor, and roof materials; building size and number of rooms.
Number and types of appliances, including ICT, lighting, and thermal comfort.
Primary cooking methods, such as firewood or traditional ovens.
Narratives linking energy to traditions, pride, or resistance to technology.
Roles in decision-making and frequency of tasks performed by men vs. women.
Desired services, perceptions of 'enough' electricity, and satisfaction levels.
Environmental awareness and narratives on saving electricity or waste reduction.
Frequency of energy-sharing and norms of reciprocity or mutual aid.
Main energy-related activities inside and outside the household.
Activity timing (morning, evening, seasonal) and time windows of use.
Split between productive activities (agriculture) and household tasks.
User narratives on appliance importance and constraints on kWh usage.
